user_query:
I'm an environmental studies major preparing a field report and I want to include clear visuals (maps, charts) in my write-up. Before I invest more time, please verify that my local environment can generate basic static figures and document the setup so I can cite it in my methods. This workspace includes a small checker script and a draft planning document.

What I need you to do:
1) Modify the configuration file at config/visuals.json so that:
   - title is set to: "Visuals Check: Rendering OK"
   - output is set to: "out/test_plot.svg"
   (You may keep the other fields as-is. The output path must be exactly out/test_plot.svg so I can reference it reliably.)

2) Run a validation command to exercise the checker and capture a machine-readable status:
   - Run: python tools/vischeck.py --config config/visuals.json --report out/vischeck_report.json
   - This should generate the SVG at out/test_plot.svg and a JSON status report at out/vischeck_report.json.

3) Compose a concise system visuals readiness summary for my project at out/system_visuals_status.md with these sections:
   - Environment check: include the Python version and working directory from out/vischeck_report.json.
   - Test artifact: include the output path and file size (in bytes) from out/vischeck_report.json, and the config title you set.
   - Next steps for project visuals: 2–4 bullet points focused on how I can incorporate visuals into my research workflow (e.g., exporting static figures, embedding in reports, maintaining config), grounded in the status results.
   The summary must be consistent with out/vischeck_report.json and reference out/test_plot.svg by its exact path.

4) Edit the existing document report/visuals_plan.md by appending a new "Verification" subsection at the end that:
   - States that the system can render static figures.
   - References the generated out/test_plot.svg and cites the configured title and the Python version from out/vischeck_report.json.
   - Keep the rest of the document intact.

Deliverables I will check:
- Modified config/visuals.json reflecting the required title and output path.
- out/test_plot.svg produced by running the checker.
- out/vischeck_report.json created by the validation command.
- out/system_visuals_status.md containing the specified sections and values drawn from the JSON report.
- Updated report/visuals_plan.md with the appended "Verification" subsection referencing the new artifacts.

Please do not use the internet. Keep all outputs within the out/ directory where specified. All reported values must be consistent with out/vischeck_report.json and the actual SVG file that was generated. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
